mission.title: containerd: Failed to exec into the container due to permission issue after executing 'systemctl daemon-reload'
mission.description: Failed to exec into the container due to permission issue after executing 'systemctl daemon-reload'. Users encounter: "Internal error occurred: error executing command in container: failed to exec in container: failed t".

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `Internal error occurred: error executing command in container: failed to exec in container: failed to start exec "d54b2d`

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
I have met a weird probelm that after I executing 'systemctl daemon-reload' on any node inside my cluster, I can't exec into those pods on that node any more.

The error message shows that it is related to a permission issue.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Failed to exec into the container due to permission issue…
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
# runc --version
runc version 1.1.3
commit: v1.1.3-0-g6724737f
spec: 1.0.2-dev
go: go1.17.10
libseccomp: 2.5.4
```

steps.title: Confirm Failed to exec into the container due to… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `Internal error occurred: error executing command in container: failed to exec in container: failed to start exec "d54b2d` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/7219

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
